name: Castillo de Gélida, Font de la Saborida – Circular Route
name_original: Castillo de Gélida, Font de la Saborida – Ruta Circular
tips: The trail is almost non-existent and steep, overgrown with brambles, but once you get to the top the views are worth it. Impossible to run in this section, I did it walking.

Located on the road from Gelida to Corbera in a section that belongs to the term of Castellví. Consisting of a stone wall with a triangular profile where you can see two circles of whiter stone that frame the two springs. Near the sink there is a curious rock converted into a seat where he prays for the oldest. The date 1932 appears on the wall of the fountain. Although it is far from urban centers, the ease of its access, next to the road, and the quality and freshness of the water make it a fairly busy place where there are usually people. filling bottles

Gelida Castle is a half-ruined castle with an adjacent church, dedicated to Saint Peter, located southeast of the urban center of Gelida, in the province of Barcelona, Spain. The church, documented as early as 945, is the only building that remains standing. It is currently part of a museum space managed by the Association of Friends of the Castle of Gelida.

The set of buildings that are part of the castle is located on a hill with different heights (250-290 meters), sought by other mountains in the north of the Sierra del Ordal. This hill is delimited by the torrents of Cantillepa to the north and San Miguel to the south. The shape of the walled enclosure is from southeast to northwest, with the highest part to the south-west.
